items.headline: Major commercial project coming to West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking significant new business activity in San José's west side. This is the largest permit in the batch and represents meaningful economic development for the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is rolling out an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses in shopping centers get compliant without penalties. This could affect local retail and small businesses operating in Cupertino shopping areas.

items.headline: City announces economic incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing new incentive programs targeting retail businesses, small businesses, and startups. This represents a direct effort to boost the local economy and business community in the South Bay.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up approved as annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored event and authorized accepting donations. Residents can participate in organized community cleanup efforts.

items.headline: Community development grant funding applicants being presented
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will hear presentations from applicants seeking federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. CDBG funds support affordable housing, social services, and community development projects.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study San Antonio Road area development and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hold a study session on land use and transportation alternatives for the San Antonio Road area, examining how the corridor could be developed or redesigned. This could affect how traffic, housing, and commercial activity are managed in that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: New retail vitality rules proposed to strengthen struggling shopping areas
items.summary: The Commission is considering changes to zoning and building codes aimed at implementing retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. The ordinance amendment could affect what types of businesses can open, how buildings are designed, and which areas the city prioritizes for commercial development.

items.headline: New electrical transmission lines coming to wastewater facility
items.summary: LS Power Grid California will install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project may affect utility services or future development in the area.
